subject: Huge water bill — I think I have a leak
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm a long-time resident at 714 Bellwood Ave and my December water bill came in at $318, which is nearly four times what I normally pay. I had a plumber out last week and he confirmed a slow leak in the line between my meter and the house that I honestly had no idea about. I got it repaired right away. Is there any kind of leak-adjustment process the village offers? I can provide the plumber's invoice. I really can't absorb a bill this size.

subject: Huge water bill — I think I have a leak
body_clean: Ms. Holt, thank you for reaching out to the Mayor's office. I'm Lorena Diaz in Water Billing. We do have a one-time leak-adjustment policy for residential accounts where a verified repair has been made. Please email me a copy of the plumber's invoice and repair summary at your earliest convenience. Once I receive those, I can apply for a credit of up to 50% of the excess usage on the affected billing cycle. You can reach me directly at Village Hall. We'll get this sorted out for you.

subject: Huge water bill — I think I have a leak
body_clean: Ms. Diaz, thank you so much — I just scanned and sent over the invoice from Alvarez Plumbing dated January 9th. The repair was a cracked copper supply line, about eighteen inches underground. I truly appreciate the village having this kind of policy. It's a relief to know there's a process for situations like this.

subject: Water bill wrong again
body_clean: Mayor. Bill says I used 18,400 gallons in September. That's impossible. I live alone. Meter must be bad. Same problem two years ago. I want someone to come read it in person. 1815 Marshall Ave.

subject: Water bill wrong again
body_clean: Mr. Kowalski, I'm scheduling a manual meter read at 1815 Marshall Ave for Thursday, October 16th between 9 a.m. and noon. A Public Works crew member will be present. If the read differs significantly from the automated figure, we'll reissue the bill. We'll also check for a possible meter fault. Please don't pay the disputed amount until we've confirmed the correct reading.

subject: Transferring water service — moving in March
body_clean: Hello, I'm purchasing the home at 408 Harvard Ave and closing on March 28th. I want to make sure the water account gets transferred into my name as of the closing date. The current owner told me service is through the Village. Can you tell me who to contact to set this up? I want to avoid any gap or confusion with billing. Thank you.

subject: Transferring water service — moving in March
body_clean: Mr. Obi, congratulations on your new home. To transfer the water account at 408 Harvard Ave into your name, please bring a copy of your closing documents to the Water Billing window at Village Hall, 3200 Washington Blvd, anytime Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. We'll do a meter read as of your closing date and open the new account same day. You'll receive a pro-rated bill for your first partial billing cycle. Welcome to Bellwood.

subject: Senior water discount — do we qualify?
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I'm 74 years old and my husband Walter is 77. We've lived at 2209 Monroe St since 1981. Our neighbor mentioned that seniors might be eligible for a reduced water rate. Neither of us is working and we're both on fixed income. Could you please let me know if such a program exists and what we'd need to apply? We always pay on time but the bills have really crept up.

subject: Senior water discount — do we qualify?
body_clean: Mrs. Szczepanski, thank you for writing. Bellwood does offer a Senior Utility Assistance discount for residents 65 and older who meet income guidelines — it provides a 15% reduction on the water and sewer portion of your bill. To apply, please bring a photo ID showing your date of birth and your most recent water bill to the Water Billing window at Village Hall. The application takes about ten minutes. If you and Walter are both on the account, either one of you can apply. Please don't hesitate to call if you need help getting here.

subject: Final bill dispute — moved out in December
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I sold my home at 619 Granville Ave and closed December 5th. I received a final water bill of $412.80 which seemed high for a partial month. I requested an itemized breakdown in December and never heard back. My new address is out of state and I'd really like to resolve this before it goes to any kind of collection. The bill covers November 1 through December 5 — that's only 35 days. Can someone please look into this?

subject: Final bill dispute — moved out in December
body_clean: Mr. Beaumont, I apologize that your December request fell through the cracks. I've pulled the account for 619 Granville Ave. The $412.80 final bill reflects 35 days of usage plus a carried balance of $118.40 from the prior cycle — the actual usage charge for the partial month is $294.40, which works out to roughly normal for that property's history. I'll email you the full itemized breakdown today. If you'd like to dispute the prior-cycle balance, please respond and I'll open a review. We will not send this to collections until the dispute window has closed.

subject: Final bill dispute — moved out in December
body_clean: Ms. Diaz, thank you for the explanation. That clears things up — I wasn't aware of the prior balance. I'll go ahead and pay the full $412.80 this week via the online portal. I appreciate you getting back to me and making sure collections was paused. That was the right thing to do.

subject: Question about my first bill at new address
body_clean: Hi there, I just moved into 1124 Frederick Ave at the beginning of March. I got my first water bill this week and I want to make sure I understand it correctly. It shows two different rates — one for the first several thousand gallons and a higher tier above that. Is that normal? Also, is there automatic bill pay available? I'd love to set that up so I don't have to think about it each month. Thank you so much.

subject: Question about my first bill at new address
body_clean: Ms. Nambiar, welcome to Bellwood and to 1124 Frederick Ave. The tiered rate structure you're seeing is correct — Bellwood uses a two-tier system where the first 6,000 gallons per billing cycle are billed at the base rate, and anything above that is billed at the conservation rate, which is slightly higher. Most households stay in the lower tier. For automatic bank-draft payments, visit the online payment portal at the Village website and choose "AutoPay Enrollment" — you'll need your account number, which appears in the top right of your bill. Any other questions, don't hesitate to ask.

subject: Billed for address I don't live at anymore
body_clean: Mayor, I moved out of 2807 Madison St back in August. I gave thirty days' notice to the landlord and I called the village about the water account. Now in November I get a bill for September and October usage at that address. I don't know who's living there but it isn't me. My name shouldn't even be on this account anymore. I need this corrected and I'm not paying for water usage I had nothing to do with.

subject: Billed for address I don't live at anymore
body_clean: Mr. Whitfield, I've reviewed the account for 2807 Madison St. Our records show a stop-service request was logged in August but the account wasn't formally closed due to a processing error on our end — that's our mistake, not yours. I'm removing the September and October charges from your account today and flagging the property for an immediate new-occupant billing setup with the landlord. You will not owe anything for that period. I'm sorry for the frustration and confusion this caused.

subject: Senior discount application — follow-up
body_clean: Hello Mayor Okonkwo, I came into Village Hall on July 29th to apply for the senior water discount. The woman at the counter was very helpful and said I'd hear something within two weeks. It's been almost three weeks and I haven't received anything by mail or email. I'm 69, live at 318 St. Paul Ave, and I'm wondering if my application went through. I have the copy they gave me if you need it.

subject: Senior discount application — follow-up
body_clean: Ms. Reyes, I found your application and I can see it was approved on August 5th but the confirmation letter was sent to an outdated mailing address in our system — again, our error. Your 15% senior discount has been active since August 1st and will appear on your next bill. I'm also correcting the address in your account right now. Thank you for following up — and I'm sorry for the confusion the delay caused.

subject: Meter reading seems impossible — nobody was home
body_clean: I'm writing because my November bill says the meter was read on November 14th between 10 a.m. and noon. I work from home and nobody knocked on my door that day. I don't have an outdoor-accessible meter box — you have to go through the basement. How exactly did they read it? I'm at 533 Hirsch Ave and my bill jumped by about $60 from last month. I want to know what actually happened.

subject: Can I set up a payment plan for an overdue balance?
body_clean: Hi, I fell behind on my water bill over the winter — I had some unexpected medical bills and things got tight. My account shows a past-due balance of $584 at my address, 2105 Washington Blvd. I'm back on my feet now and absolutely want to pay this, I just can't do it all at once. Is there any kind of installment plan available? I don't want service interrupted. Thank you.

subject: Can I set up a payment plan for an overdue balance?
body_clean: Thank you for reaching out proactively — that goes a long way. Yes, we offer a hardship payment plan that allows you to spread a past-due balance over up to six monthly installments while keeping your service active, provided you stay current on new charges going forward. For your $584 balance, that could be as low as $97.34 per month on top of your regular bill. Please stop by Water Billing at Village Hall or call us to sign the agreement — we just need it in writing to activate the plan. Service will not be interrupted while the plan is in effect.

subject: Water account — selling home, need final read
body_clean: Good afternoon, I'm in the process of selling my home at 927 Eastern Ave and my closing is scheduled for October 11th. My real estate attorney is asking for a final water meter reading and any outstanding balance as of the closing date. How do I request that? I've lived here twenty-two years and I want to make sure everything is settled cleanly for the new buyer.
